channel.title: Craft
channel.description: Craft is a list of hand oriented craft and technology Do we really need all those power tools?

item.title: Revive or Restore a Finish?
item.description:
Discover how (and when) to give old, deteriorated finishes new life. As finishes age, they deteriorate. First they dull, then they begin showing small cracks (called “crazing”). The culprit of...

Source
item.source:markdown:
![](https://media.popularwoodworking.com/app/uploads/2020/04/16131246/01pwm1308flexner-1024x473.jpg)Discover how (and when) to give old, deteriorated finishes new life. As finishes age, they deteriorate. First they dull, then they begin showing small cracks (called “crazing”). The culprit of...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/finishing/flexner-on-finsihing-revive-or-restore/)

item.title: Mind Upon Mind: Stick Stop
item.description: Editor’s note: Our Mind Upon Mind series is a nod to a 1937 Chips from the Chisel column (also featured in “Honest Labour: The Charles H. Hayward Years”), in which Hayward wrote, “The influence of mind upon mind is extraordinary.” The idea being there’s often room for improvement. To that end, we’ve asked you what else you have...
item.source:markdown: Editor’s note: Our Mind Upon Mind series is a nod to a 1937 Chips from the Chisel column (also featured in “Honest Labour: The Charles H. Hayward Years”), in which Hayward wrote, “The influence of mind upon mind is extraordinary.” The idea being there’s often room for improvement. To that end, we’ve asked you what else you have...

item.title: The Splinter Report: April 24th
item.description:

item.title: Devilishly Clever ‘Doe’s Foot’
item.description:
These notched sticks simplify your workholding at the bench. Workbenches didn’t always have vises. In fact, for most of human history, workbenches around the world were nothing more than a...

Source
item.source:markdown:
![](https://media.popularwoodworking.com/app/uploads/2020/09/16125658/doefoot_cs_opener_img_4716-2-copy1-1024x768.jpg)These notched sticks simplify your workholding at the bench. Workbenches didn’t always have vises. In fact, for most of human history, workbenches around the world were nothing more than a...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/techniques/devilishly-clever-foot/)

item.title: Acanthus Leaf
item.description:
Learn how to carve this classic detail – follow a few rules, and it’s simple. The beauty and elegance of the acanthus leaf has inspired artists, architects and craftsmen for...

Source

Hayward Years”), in which Hayward wrote, “The influence of mind upon mind is extraordinary.” The idea being there’s often room for improvement. To that end, we’ve asked you what else you have...

Hayward Years”), in which Hayward wrote, “The influence of mind upon mind is extraordinary.” The idea being there’s often room for improvement. To that end, we’ve asked you what else you have...

item.title: The $15 Lab Test: A Modern Take on the Ryoba Saw
item.description:
In my woodworking classes, our tool rack is a bit like a stress-test laboratory. From fourth grade through high school, my students use Japanese-style saws for nearly every project. These...

Source
item.source:markdown:

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/toolreviews/the-15-lab-test-a-modern-take-on-the-ryoba-saw/)

item.title: ShopNotes Podcast Episode 268 — A Fistful of Pearwood
item.description:
In this episode, we’re finding a woodworking fix for invasive Callery pear trees and catching up with the Woodsmith Shop and Popular Woodworking. Head to the Woodsmith website for more...

Head to the Woodsmith website for more...

item.title: LAP Open Wire, April 18, 2026
item.description: Comments are now closed – thanks to everyone who joined us today, and those reading later. Join us again on June 20! Got a woodworking question, a query about dog holes (which Kale is drilling above, for their new tail vise) or are curious to know what’s on the horizon at Lost Art Press? Today...
item.source:markdown: Comments are now closed – thanks to everyone who joined us today, and those reading later. Join us again on June 20! Got a woodworking question, a query about dog holes (which Kale is drilling above, for their new tail vise) or are curious to know what’s on the horizon at Lost Art Press? Today...

item.title: Tool + Accessory = A Better Tool
item.description:
Milwaukee has added more offerings in its ever-growing category of vacuum tool accessories, with a couple of them great for woodworkers. The company’s “Air-Tip” tools have always been targeted at the...

Source
item.source:markdown:
![](https://media.popularwoodworking.com/app/uploads/2026/04/16130516/ajblog-1245-image-copy-1024x578.jpg)Milwaukee has added more offerings in its ever-growing category of vacuum tool accessories, with a couple of them great for woodworkers. The company’s “Air-Tip” tools have always been targeted at the...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/toolreviews/tool-accessory-a-better-tool/)

item.title: Slow Cooker
item.description: I’ve been at home this week (and possibly will be next) staring on and off at my not-yet-finished kitchen – on which I cannot currently work – every time I go to refresh my ice pack and drink more fluids. (I’m recovering from minor but uncomfortable surgery, and am under doctor’s orders to do no...
item.source:markdown: I’ve been at home this week (and possibly will be next) staring on and off at my not-yet-finished kitchen – on which I cannot currently work – every time I go to refresh my ice pack and drink more fluids. (I’m recovering from minor but uncomfortable surgery, and am under doctor’s orders to do no...

item.title: Simple Router Table
item.description:
Easy to make, easy to store. While many woodworkers spend weekends making stationary router tables, mine have always been very simple, driven by expediency, and the desire to get other...

Source
item.source:markdown:
![](https://media.popularwoodworking.com/app/uploads/2020/01/aw_14640_012-1024x768.jpg)Easy to make, easy to store. While many woodworkers spend weekends making stationary router tables, mine have always been very simple, driven by expediency, and the desire to get other...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/projects/simple-router-table/)

item.title: A Back-saving Vise Extension
item.description:
While sawing dovetails for a jewelry box, I found my back was hurting from hunching over my bench vise. In order to work more comfortably, I built this vise extension...

Source
item.source:markdown:
![](https://media.popularwoodworking.com/app/uploads/2026/04/16104634/22223-1024x768.jpg)While sawing dovetails for a jewelry box, I found my back was hurting from hunching over my bench vise. In order to work more comfortably, I built this vise extension...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/tricks/a-back-saving-vise-extension/)

item.title: Floating Top Entry Table
item.description:
This mitered case and veneered top make this project an interesting challenge. This floating top entry table was a project that I designed and built for the 2025 Grizzly Makers...

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/article/floating-top-entry-table/)

Founded by Luke Lyu, Zen-Wu...

item.title: Ripping Thin Pieces
item.description:
While making Shaker boxes, I built a jig to accurately rip 1/16″ thick strips without getting my hands too close to the blade. In fact, I can leave the guard...

Source

![](https://media.popularwoodworking.com/app/uploads/2020/09/tips_sip2010_003-1024x683.jpg)While making Shaker boxes, I built a jig to accurately rip 1/16″ thick strips without getting my hands too close to the blade. In fact, I can leave the guard...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/tricks/ripping-thin-pieces/)

item.title: AI in the Shop, Part 4: The Digital Shop Manager & Tool Builder
item.description:
In the final chapter of this experiment, the technology has finally found its permanent place in my shop—not at the workbench, but at the desk. For the solo maker, AI’s...

Source

item.title: Applications Open for Chairmaker’s Toolbox Class
item.description: The Chairmaker’s Toolbox is accepting applications for a scholarship class this August at Lost Art Press. All six students will receive free tuition and materials (plus some meals) during the week-long chairmaking class, Aug. 3-7. Apply for this scholarship class here. Applications close on May 15. The class will be taught by Grace Ambrose and...
item.source:markdown: The Chairmaker’s Toolbox is accepting applications for a scholarship class this August at Lost Art Press. All six students will receive free tuition and materials (plus some meals) during the week-long chairmaking class, Aug. 3-7. Apply for this scholarship class here. Applications close on May 15. The class will be taught by Grace Ambrose and...

item.title: See My Chairs at the ‘No Coast Furniture Salone’
item.description: I’m exhibiting two stick chairs at the “No Coast Furniture Salone” that runs from Friday, April 17, through Sunday, May 3, in an exhibit space at 1800 Race Street in Cincinnati, Ohio. I’m showing two stick chairs in black cherry that are based on the 18th-century Bebb chair from Wales (one of the key chairs...
item.source:markdown: I’m exhibiting two stick chairs at the “No Coast Furniture Salone” that runs from Friday, April 17, through Sunday, May 3, in an exhibit space at 1800 Race Street in Cincinnati, Ohio. I’m showing two stick chairs in black cherry that are based on the 18th-century Bebb chair from Wales (one of the key chairs...

item.title: Production With a Purpose
item.description:
A national nonprofit, Sleep in Heavenly Peace, organizes “build days” where volunteers construct beds for children in need. Watching the news can be depressing, except when the story is how...

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/editors-blog/production-with-a-purpose/)

item.title: Charred Finish
item.description:
Fire and wax together produce a rich black surface. Fire might not be the first finishing technique that most woodworkers gravitate toward; then again, I am not most woodworkers. As...

[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/finishing/charred-finish/)

item.title: Bur Oak
item.description:

item.title: Super Sandpaper
item.description:
Whenever I was sanding by hand, the sandpaper would roll or slide in my hands, eventually tearing and turning into small, useless scraps. I tried folding it every which way...

Source
item.source:markdown:
![](https://media.popularwoodworking.com/app/uploads/2026/04/06174408/mar07-super-sandpaper-copy-1024x667.jpg)Whenever I was sanding by hand, the sandpaper would roll or slide in my hands, eventually tearing and turning into small, useless scraps. I tried folding it every which way...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/tricks/super-sandpaper-2/)
